en: Paul came also to Derbe and to Lystra. A disciple was there, named Timothy, the son of a Jewish woman who was a believer, but his father was a Greek.
zh: 保羅來到了特庇，又到了路司得。在那裡有一個門徒，名叫提摩太，是一個信主的猶太婦人的兒子，父親卻是希臘人。

en: He was well spoken of by the brothers at Lystra and Iconium.
zh: 路司得和以哥念的弟兄們都稱讚他。

en: Paul wanted Timothy to accompany him, and he took him and circumcised him because of the Jews who were in those places, for they all knew that his father was a Greek.
zh: 保羅有意要他一同出去，但為了那些地方的猶太人，就給他行了割禮，因為他們都知道他父親是希臘人。

en: As they went on their way through the cities, they delivered to them for observance the decisions that had been reached by the apostles and elders who were in Jerusalem.
zh: 他們經過各城，把耶路撒冷的使徒和長老所定的規條，交給門徒遵守。

en: So the churches were strengthened in the faith, and they increased in numbers daily.
zh: 於是眾教會信心越發堅固，人數天天增加。

en: And they went through the region of Phrygia and Galatia, having been forbidden by the Holy Spirit to speak the word in Asia.
zh: 聖靈既然禁止他們在亞西亞傳道，他們就走遍弗呂家、加拉太地區。

en: And when they had come up to Mysia, they attempted to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus did not allow them.
zh: 他們來到每西亞邊境，想要去庇推尼，耶穌的靈也不許。

en: So, passing by Mysia, they went down to Troas.
zh: 他們經過每西亞，下到特羅亞。

en: And a vision appeared to Paul in the night: a man of Macedonia was standing there, urging him and saying, "Come over to Macedonia and help us."
zh: 夜間有一個異象向保羅顯現：有一個馬其頓人站著求他說：“請你到馬其頓來，幫助我們！”

en: And when Paul had seen the vision, immediately we sought to go on into Macedonia, concluding that God had called us to preach the gospel to them.
zh: 保羅見了這異象，我們就認定是 神呼召我們去傳福音給他們，於是立刻設法前往馬其頓。

en: So, setting sail from Troas, we made a direct voyage to Samothrace, and the following day to Neapolis,
zh: 我們從特羅亞開船，直航撒摩特拉，第二天到達尼亞波利，

en: and from there to Philippi, which is a leading city of the district of Macedonia and a Roman colony. We remained in this city some days.
zh: 從那裡來到腓立比，就是馬其頓地區的首要城市，是羅馬的殖民地。我們在這城裡住了幾天。

en: And on the Sabbath day we went outside the gate to the riverside, where we supposed there was a place of prayer, and we sat down and spoke to the women who had come together.
zh: 安息日我們出了城門，來到河邊，以為那裡是個祈禱的地方。我們坐下，對聚集的婦女講論。

en: One who heard us was a woman named Lydia, from the city of Thyatira, a seller of purple goods, who was a worshiper of God. The Lord opened her heart to pay attention to what was said by Paul.
zh: 有一位敬畏 神的婦女，名叫呂底亞，是推雅推拉城賣紫色布的商人，她一直在聽，主開啟她的心，使她留心聽保羅所講的。

en: And after she was baptized, and her household as well, she urged us, saying, "If you have judged me to be faithful to the Lord, come to my house and stay." And she prevailed upon us.
zh: 她和她一家受了洗以後，就請求說：“你們若認為我是對主忠實的，就請到我家來住。”於是她強留我們。

en: As we were going to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl who had a spirit of divination and brought her owners much gain by fortune-telling.
zh: 有一次，我們到祈禱的地方去的時候，一個被巫鬼附著的婢女迎面而來；她行占卜使主人們發了大財。

en: She followed Paul and us, crying out, "These men are servants of the Most High God, who proclaim to you the way of salvation."
zh: 她跟著保羅和我們，喊叫說：“這些人是至高 神的僕人，向你們傳講得救的道路。”

en: And this she kept doing for many days. Paul, having become greatly annoyed, turned and said to the spirit, "I command you in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her." And it came out that very hour.
zh: 她一連多日這樣喊叫，保羅覺得厭煩，就轉身對那鬼說：“我奉耶穌基督的名，命令你從她身上出來！”那鬼就立刻出來了。

en: But when her owners saw that their hope of gain was gone, they seized Paul and Silas and dragged them into the marketplace before the rulers.
zh: 她的主人們看見發財的希望完了，就揪住保羅和西拉，拉到市中心去見官長，

en: And when they had brought them to the magistrates, they said, "These men are Jews, and they are disturbing our city.
zh: 又帶到裁判官面前，說：“這些人是猶太人，擾亂我們的城市，

en: They advocate customs that are not lawful for us as Romans to accept or practice."
zh: 傳我們羅馬人不准接受、不准實行的規例。”

en: The crowd joined in attacking them, and the magistrates tore the garments off them and gave orders to beat them with rods.
zh: 群眾一齊起來攻擊他們，裁判官就剝去他們的衣服，下令用棍子打他們。

en: And when they had inflicted many blows upon them, they threw them into prison, ordering the jailer to keep them safely.
zh: 打了很多棍，就把他們放在監牢裡，吩咐獄吏嚴密看守。

en: Having received this order, he put them into the inner prison and fastened their feet in the stocks.
zh: 獄吏領了命令，就把他們押入內監，兩腳拴了木狗。

en: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the prisoners were listening to them,
zh: 約在半夜，保羅和西拉祈禱歌頌 神，囚犯們都側耳聽著。

en: and suddenly there was a great earthquake, so that the foundations of the prison were shaken. And immediately all the doors were opened, and everyone's bonds were unfastened.
zh: 忽然發生了大地震，以致監牢的地基都搖動起來，所有的監門立刻開了，囚犯的鎖鍊都鬆了。

en: When the jailer woke and saw that the prison doors were open, he drew his sword and was about to kill himself, supposing that the prisoners had escaped.
zh: 獄吏醒過來，看見監門全開，以為囚犯都已經逃脫了，就拔出刀來想要自刎。

en: But Paul cried with a loud voice, "Do not harm yourself, for we are all here."
zh: 保羅大聲呼叫說：“不要傷害自己，我們都在這裡！”

en: And the jailer called for lights and rushed in, and trembling with fear he fell down before Paul and Silas.
zh: 獄吏叫人拿了燈來，就衝進去，戰戰兢兢地俯伏在保羅和西拉面前，

en: Then he brought them out and said, "Sirs, what must I do to be saved?"
zh: 隨後領他們出來，說：“先生，我應該作甚麼才可以得救？”

en: And they said, "Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved, you and your household."
zh: 他們說：“當信主耶穌，你和你一家人都必定得救。”

en: And they spoke the word of the Lord to him and to all who were in his house.
zh: 他們就把主的道，講給他和所有在他家裡的人聽。

en: And he took them the same hour of the night and washed their wounds; and he was baptized at once, he and all his family.
zh: 就在當夜的那個時候，獄吏領他們去洗傷，獄吏和他家人都受了洗，

en: Then he brought them up into his house and set food before them. And he rejoiced along with his entire household that he had believed in God.
zh: 就帶他們到家裡，擺上飯食，他和全家因信了 神就大大喜樂。

en: But when it was day, the magistrates sent the police, saying, "Let those men go."
zh: 到了天亮，裁判官派法警來，說：“放了這些人！”

en: And the jailer reported these words to Paul, saying, "The magistrates have sent to let you go. Therefore come out now and go in peace."
zh: 獄吏就把這話告訴保羅，說：“裁判官派人來釋放你們，現在可以出來，平平安安地去吧！”

en: But Paul said to them, "They have beaten us publicly, uncondemned, men who are Roman citizens, and have thrown us into prison; and do they now throw us out secretly? No! Let them come themselves and take us out."
zh: 保羅對他們說：“我們是羅馬人，還沒有定罪，他們就公開打我們，又放在監裡；現在要私下趕我們出去嗎？不行！他們應當親自來，領我們出去！”

en: The police reported these words to the magistrates, and they were afraid when they heard that they were Roman citizens.
zh: 法警把這番話回報裁判官，裁判官聽說他們是羅馬人，就害怕起來，

en: So they came and apologized to them. And they took them out and asked them to leave the city.
zh: 於是來請求他們，領他們出監之後，就請他們離開那城。

en: So they went out of the prison and visited Lydia. And when they had seen the brothers, they encouraged them and departed.
zh: 兩人出了監，就到呂底亞的家裡去，見了弟兄們，勸勉他們一番，就離開了。
